question: Looking at the unemployment rate map, which statement below is the most accurate?

options.A: Georgia and Pennsylvania likely have unemployment rates within a similar range.
options.B: Illinois and Connecticut likely have unemployment rates within a similar range.
options.C: Idaho likely has a much lower unemployment rate than Wyoming.
options.D: The unemployment rate differences between any two states cannot be compared using this map.

explanation: Georgia (9.7%) and Pennsylvania (12.7%) have a 3.0% unemployment rate difference, while Illinois (5.6%) and Idaho (6.3%) have only a 0.7% difference. However, the categorical color encoding groups Georgia and Pennsylvania into the same color bin (8%+ range), making their large difference appear similar, while Illinois and Idaho appear more different due to different color bins.
